assertions.expectation: Clearly states that GKE auto-upgrades clusters automatically as the default and recommended model — this is what differentiates GKE from self-managed Kubernetes
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'GKE handles upgrades automatically — this is the recommended approach' and explains this is better than manual management

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows to control WHEN auto-upgrades happen (e.g., weekends, off-peak hours)
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command for configuring maintenance windows with off-peak hours and weekly recurrence

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance exclusions to control WHAT auto-upgrades happen (e.g., 'no minor or node upgrades' to block disruptive changes while allowing security patches)
assertions.reason: Shows example of 'no_minor_upgrades' exclusion with gcloud command to control which types of upgrades are allowed

assertions.expectation: Does NOT recommend disabling auto-upgrades or switching to a purely manual upgrade cadence as the first option
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends auto-upgrades and positions manual upgrades as exceptions for specific scenarios only

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades are useful for specific scenarios (emergency patches, accelerating the schedule) but are the exception, not the rule
assertions.reason: Has dedicated section 'When to Use Manual Upgrades' listing specific scenarios like accelerating security patches and testing, but positions them as exceptions

assertions.expectation: Recommends enrolling in scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance) for visibility into upcoming auto-upgrades
assertions.reason: Mentions enabling cluster notifications via Pub/Sub to get alerts when upgrades start/complete in the Next Steps section

assertions.expectation: Suggests checking the GKE release schedule for longer-range planning (~1 month best-case for new minors)
assertions.reason: Does not mention checking GKE release schedules or provide timing information for longer-range planning of minor version releases

assertions.expectation: Mentions release channel selection as the primary lever for upgrade cadence (Stable = slower, Regular = balanced)
assertions.reason: Acknowledges they're on Regular channel but doesn't explain how different release channels (Stable, Regular, Rapid) affect upgrade cadence or suggest this as a primary control mechanism
